title: agrostis scabra subsp. septentrionalis
intro: Agrostis scabra subsp. septentrionalis, also known as the rough bluegrass or northern bluegrass, is a subspecies of grass within the genus Agrostis, native to the boreal regions of North America. Traditionally used in herbal medicine, this plant has been found to possess antimicrobial properties, which can aid in the treatment of various infections and wounds. The health benefits of Agrostis scabra subsp. septentrionalis include its potential to reduce inflammation, improve respiratory issues, and support the immune system. Herbal preparations of this plant can be made into teas, infusions, or tinctures, which can be consumed to harness its medicinal properties.
